meta.title: Memory for the Future
meta.description: A digital memorial preserving the memory and lessons of disaster-affected areas

timeline.subtitle: A chronicle of Namie Town, beginning March 11, 2011

lessons.title: What This Disaster Taught Us
lessons.subtitle: Lessons for the future, so tragedy is never repeated

initiatives.title: Namie Today
initiatives.subtitle: The ongoing efforts to rebuild Namie Town

garmentBridge.title: What This Garment Carries
garmentBridge.narrative: To keep this memory alive in everyday life. YURA's reversible t-shirt carries the beauty of recovery on the outside, and the memory of disaster when turned inside-out. Through the QR code, you access this archive — the wearer becomes a storyteller of memory.

action.title: Carry the Memory Forward
action.subtitle: Learning is the first action. Sharing is the next step.
action.shareText: Memories we must not forget. Learn the story of this place.

share.title: Share
share.description: Share this memorial to spread awareness

visit.title: Visit
visit.description: Visit Namie Town and experience its recovery firsthand

support.title: Support
support.description: Support Namie's recovery through Furusato Tax

footer.disclaimer: Information on this site is based on official government sources and firsthand testimonies. While we strive for accuracy, please refer to the cited sources for details.
footer.sources: Sources: Reconstruction Agency, Namie Town, Cabinet Office Disaster Prevention

reversible.title: The Act of Flipping
reversible.subtitle: The outside is renewal and hope. Turn it inside-out, and find memory and reality. Two worlds in one garment.
reversible.concept: YURA's reversible design imbues meaning into the very act of turning a t-shirt inside-out. The exterior carries the beauty of recovery; flip it to reveal the raw reality of disaster. That moment of turning becomes a gateway to forgotten memories.

front.label: Front — QR Code
front.description: A small QR code on the chest — your portal to the digital archive

back.label: Back — Landscape of Recovery
back.description: The Fukushima coastline. Nature reclaiming what was lost

inside-out.label: Inside-Out — Memory
inside-out.description: Turn it inside-out to reveal the post-disaster landscape. Memories we must not forget
